description:
## Background

`src-tauri/src/workspace.rs` contains the backend-owned Workspace Service and its largest inline Rust suite: 38 tests covering restoration, validation, catalog retention, Current Workspace commit, MRU ordering, cancellation, retries, persistence failures, and generation guards. Those tests are essential to the atomic Workspace transaction, but their placement makes the production state machine difficult to scan.

## What to build

Move all Workspace Service test bodies and local test doubles out of `workspace.rs` into behavior-focused outlined files. The production module must retain only the test-module declaration needed to compile tests under `cargo test`.

## Technical Approach

Use an outlined child module rooted at `src-tauri/src/workspace/tests.rs`; split it further only where it improves discovery, for example `selection`, `persistence`, `cancellation_and_retry`, and `commit_and_staleness`. Preserve child-module access to private Workspace Service items. Keep `FakeStore`, `FakeRunner`, and persistent test-store support local to the Workspace test module; do not make production items public or introduce a cross-crate test utility.

## Considerations

- The established Workspace vocabulary is Current Workspace, Pending Workspace, Workspace Catalog, and Unavailable Workspace.
- Preserve the exact command and persistence ordering assertions that protect atomic switching.
- This is not a change to Workspace behavior, store format, RPC, or the desktop end-to-end suite.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `workspace.rs` contains no test bodies; its tests compile from outlined files through `#[cfg(test)] mod tests;`.
- [ ] Every existing Workspace test is moved once, retaining its test name and behavior-level assertion unless a mechanical path update requires otherwise.
- [ ] Test support remains private to the Workspace test module and production visibility is unchanged.
- [ ] Focused Workspace tests and full `cargo test` pass.

## Test Plan

- Count and run the migrated Workspace tests with a focused `cargo test workspace` filter or equivalent.
- Run `cargo test` from `src-tauri`.
- Verify no `#[test]` body remains in `src-tauri/src/workspace.rs`.

## Triage

ready-for-agent
title: Outline Workspace Service tests
